User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FinancioAI application.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the user account was created.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer to whom the user sends invoices.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice sent to a customer.

issueDate.description: Date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: Date when the invoice is due.

status.description: Status of the invoice (e.g., sent, paid, overdue).

pdfUrl.description: URL of the generated invoice PDF.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents an income or expense transaction.

category.description: Category of the transaction (e.g., salary, rent, utilities).

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Only the authenticated user can read or write their own profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores customer data associated with a specific user. Only the authenticated user can manage their own customers.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Stores invoice data associated with a specific user and customer. Only the authenticated user can manage their own invoices.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice.

definition.description: Stores transaction data associated with a specific user. Only the authenticated user can manage their own transactions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, DBAC, and QAPs. It leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and denormalization where necessary to avoid using `get()` calls in security rules. The structure separates data with different access requirements into distinct collections, enhancing security and query performance.

*   **Authorization Independence**: Achieved by using path-based ownership for user-related data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/customers/{customerId}`). No `get()` calls are required in rules to validate ownership. Access to user data is governed solely by the authenticated user's ID.
*   **DBAC (Database Authenticated Access Control)**: Roles are implicitly determined by the path structure (`/users/{userId}`). No custom claims are needed; authorization relies exclusively on `request.auth.uid`.
*   **QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**: The path-based ownership structure enables secure list operations. For example, a user can only list customers under their `/users/{userId}/customers` collection, ensuring that rules don't act as filters by accidentally exposing data from other users.

Specific design decisions include:

*   Storing `Customer`, `Invoice`, and `Transaction` data as subcollections of `/users/{userId}`. This enforces clear ownership and simplifies security rules, as only the authenticated user has access to their respective data.
*   Each entity (`Customer`, `Invoice`, `Transaction`) uses a unique ID for its documents, automatically generated by Firestore.
*   Timestamp fields such as `createdAt`, `issueDate`, and `date` are stored as strings in `date-time` format, which allows for flexible date querying and sorting. For enhanced query capabilities on date ranges, consider adding `timestamp` fields as well.

This structure adheres to the core design principles, promoting secure, scalable, and debuggable Firestore data management for FinancioAI.
